panels.description: Wide shot: A giant mushroom in a hazy, dreamlike forest. The Caterpillar sits atop it, smoking a hookah. Alice stands below, looking up respectfully. Soft, diffused lighting.

bubbles.text: Repeat, 'You are old, Father William,' said the Caterpillar.

panels.description: Medium shot: A surreal imagination bubble or vignette. Father William, an old man with bushy white hair and Victorian clothes, is balancing perfectly on his head on a stone path. Alice is visible in the corner, reciting with a confused look.

bubbles.text: You are old, Father William, And your hair has become very white; And yet you incessantly stand on your head— Do you think, at your age, it is right?
In my youth, I feared it might injure the brain; But, now that I'm perfectly sure I have none, Why, I do it again and again.

panels.description: Medium shot: Father William, despite his stout frame, performs a graceful back-somersault through a cottage doorway. In the second half of the panel, he holds up a small, ornate tin box of ointment toward the viewer.

bubbles.text: You are old, as I mentioned before, And have grown most uncommonly fat; Yet you turned a back-somersault in at the door— Pray, what is the reason of that?
In my youth, I kept all my limbs very supple By the use of this ointment—one shilling the box—Allow me to sell you a couple?

panels.description: Medium close shot: Father William is shown with his mouth wide open, mid-bite into a whole roasted goose, bones and all. His jaw looks unnaturally strong and defined. A law-court setting is faintly visible in the background background.

bubbles.text: You are old, and your jaws are too weak For anything tougher than suet; Yet you finished the goose, with the bones and the beak— Pray, how did you manage to do it?
In my youth, I took to the law, And argued each case with my wife; And the muscular strength, which it gave to my jaw, Has lasted the rest of my life.

panels.description: Close up: A split panel. On one side, Father William balances a long, slippery eel on his nose with an intense focus. On the other side, he looks incredibly annoyed, pointing a finger aggressively toward the exit.

bubbles.text: You are old, one would hardly suppose Your eye was as steady as ever; Yet you balanced an eel on the end of your nose— What made you so awfully clever?
I have answered three questions, and that is enough! Don't give yourself airs! Be off, or I'll kick you down stairs!

panels.description: Medium shot: Back in the woods. The Caterpillar looks down at Alice through a cloud of smoke. Alice looks a bit flustered, smoothing her apron and looking up at the mushroom top.

bubbles.text: What size do you want to be?
I'm not particular. But one doesn't like changing so often.

panels.description: Close up: Alice's face, looking earnest and a bit sad. She is only three inches tall and looks tiny compared to the blades of grass around her. The Caterpillar's large eye is visible in the background.

bubbles.text: Well, I should like to be a little larger, sir, If you wouldn't mind, I'm afraid three inches is such a wretched height.
